Name: Unintended acceleration
Description: The vehicle accelerates without driver command due to throttle actuator malfunction, potentially leading to collision with objects or pedestrians.

Name: Loss of braking assist
Description: Electronic braking assist system fails to provide expected braking force during emergency braking, increasing stopping distance.

Name: Unintended lane departure
Description: Lane keeping assist applies incorrect steering torque causing vehicle to depart lane into oncoming traffic.

Name: False forward collision warning
Description: Forward collision warning system fails to detect obstacle ahead, providing no warning before impact.

Name: Unintended steering intervention
Description: Electric power steering system applies unintended torque to steering column during highway driving.

Name: Radar sensor blindness
Description: Front-facing radar fails to detect stationary or slow-moving vehicles due to sensor degradation or interference.

Name: Camera perception failure
Description: Camera-based perception system misclassifies pedestrian as non-hazardous object in low-light conditions.

Name: Unintended acceleration on highway
Description: Vehicle accelerates unexpectedly while traveling at highway speed (>100 km/h) in moderate traffic, driver cannot immediately override throttle.

Name: Brake assist failure during emergency stop
Description: Braking assist fails during emergency braking at 60 km/h in urban environment with pedestrian crossing ahead.

Name: Lane departure into oncoming traffic
Description: Lane keeping assist steers vehicle left across center line into oncoming traffic on a two-lane rural road at 80 km/h.

Name: Missing FCW at highway speed
Description: Forward collision warning fails to alert driver of stopped vehicle ahead while traveling at 120 km/h on highway.

Name: Steering torque during lane change
Description: EPS applies 5 Nm unintended torque during active lane change at 130 km/h on highway.

Name: Radar miss of stationary vehicle
Description: Radar fails to detect stationary vehicle in own lane while approaching at 80 km/h; no AEB trigger.

Name: Pedestrian misclassification at night
Description: Camera system classifies pedestrian on crosswalk as road furniture during nighttime driving at 50 km/h in urban area.

Name: Prevent unintended acceleration
Description: The system shall prevent unintended vehicle acceleration exceeding 0.3 m/s² without valid driver throttle input.

Name: Ensure minimum braking performance
Description: The system shall ensure braking deceleration of at least 6.5 m/s² is achievable at all times when brake pedal is applied.

Name: Prevent unsafe lane departure
Description: The system shall not apply steering torque that causes the vehicle to depart its lane into adjacent lanes with oncoming traffic.

Name: Ensure forward collision detection
Description: The system shall detect obstacles in the vehicle path and provide timely warning to the driver with sufficient time-to-collision margin (>2.5s).

Name: Limit unintended steering torque
Description: The system shall limit unintended EPS torque to less than 3 Nm and provide torque feedback consistent with driver steering input.

Name: Ensure radar detection reliability
Description: The system shall maintain radar detection capability for objects larger than 1m² at ranges up to 150m with probability of detection >99.5%.

Name: Ensure pedestrian classification accuracy
Description: The system shall correctly classify pedestrians with >99% accuracy in all lighting conditions within the operational design domain.

Name: Throttle monitoring and limitation
Description: The system shall independently monitor commanded vs. actual throttle position and cut fuel injection within 100ms if deviation exceeds 5% without driver input.

Name: Redundant throttle position sensing
Description: The system shall use two independent throttle position sensors with cross-checking; disagreement >3% shall trigger safe state within 50ms.

Name: Brake force monitoring
Description: The system shall continuously monitor brake hydraulic pressure and wheel deceleration; if brake assist output deviates >15% from commanded value, alert driver and activate backup braking within 200ms.

Name: Brake system self-test
Description: The system shall perform brake actuator self-test at each ignition cycle and disable brake assist with driver warning if test fails.

Name: Steering torque limitation
Description: The system shall limit LKA steering intervention torque to 3 Nm maximum and provide haptic override capability requiring <5 Nm driver counter-torque.

Name: Lane departure direction check
Description: The system shall verify lane departure direction against oncoming traffic detection before applying corrective steering; shall not steer toward detected oncoming vehicles.

Name: FCW minimum detection range
Description: The system shall detect and classify vehicles at minimum 120m range with time-to-collision computation updated at 20 Hz minimum.

Name: EPS torque arbitration
Description: The system shall implement torque arbitration logic ensuring driver steering input always takes priority over automated steering commands within 20ms.

Name: Radar performance monitoring
Description: The system shall continuously monitor radar signal quality metrics (SNR, clutter level, target consistency) and degrade gracefully with driver notification when performance falls below threshold.

Name: Multi-sensor pedestrian fusion
Description: The system shall fuse camera and radar data for pedestrian detection using independent classification algorithms; disagreement between sensors shall trigger conservative (assume-pedestrian) behavior.

Name: Throttle monitoring response test
Description: Verify throttle monitoring cuts fuel injection within 100ms when throttle deviation >5% is injected.

Name: Redundant sensor disagreement test
Description: Inject 4% disagreement between throttle sensors and verify safe state activation within 50ms.

Name: Brake assist deviation response
Description: Simulate 20% brake force deviation and verify driver alert + backup braking activation within 200ms.

Name: LKA torque override test
Description: Apply 6 Nm driver counter-torque during active LKA intervention and verify system yields within 50ms.

Name: FCW detection range validation
Description: Present target vehicle at 120m range and verify detection and TTC computation at 20 Hz update rate.
